text:
It was a strange thing about Mrs. Barnaby. Every morning she would awaken with a peculiar singing voice, reminiscent of a robin with a love for opera.

One day she learned that she was a secret soprano. It wasn't every day that someone discovered a talent like hers. She called it "serenading the sows."

People came from miles around just to hear her sing. Eventually she got her own radio show. She'd talk about things, occasionally singing about them too.

A lot of people wanted to meet her, but Mrs. Barnaby said she wasn't really interesting. All she did was sing. She was busy raising kids.

She never left town. People did visit, though. They brought her all sorts of songs. She liked them, except for those silly old ballads about knights.

Every time a knight sang to a lady, she'd pretend to swoon. "That's just our little secret," she'd say conspiratorially.

They say Mrs.
